name: Remote Terminal (Node.js Agent)
description: Node.js agent that exposes a local shell over NoLag for remote terminal access

topics.description: Commands received from the web client (shell, session_start, session_input, etc.)

topics.description: Command responses sent back to the web client

topics.name: session_output
topics.description: Interactive PTY session output streamed to the web client

readme:
# Remote Terminal (Node.js Agent)

## Description
A Node.js agent that runs on a target machine and exposes its shell over NoLag. Supports one-shot shell commands, interactive PTY sessions, file transfer, tab completion, and directory listing.

This is the **Node.js agent** component of a multi-part system. To use it, you also need the web client:
- [remote-terminal-web](../remote-terminal-web/) -- Browser-based terminal client

Alternative agents are available in other languages:
- [remote-terminal](../remote-terminal/) -- Go agent + TUI client
- [remote-terminal-python](../remote-terminal-python/) -- Python agent

## Getting Started
1. Download or clone this blueprint to the target machine
2. Install dependencies: `npm install`
3. Build: `npm run build`
4. Run the agent:
   ```
   node dist/index.js --token <YOUR_TOKEN> --apikey <YOUR_API_KEY> --appid <YOUR_APP_ID>
   ```
5. Open the web client and connect using the device ID

## Architecture
- Subscribes to the `commands` topic on a room named after the device ID
- Executes commands locally using `child_process` or `node-pty` for interactive sessions
- Streams PTY output back via the `session_output` topic
- Broadcasts device status every 30 seconds on the `status` topic
- Auto-creates the room via the NoLag REST API on startup

## NoLag Features Used
- **Pub/Sub messaging** for command/response exchange
- **Room-per-device** with dynamic room creation via REST API
- **Reconnection** for resilient long-running agent connections
- **Binary-safe messaging** via base64 encoding for PTY data
